message:
test(db/cache): proptest for interleaved dual-index cache coherency

Add a property-based test that hammers the agent cache's by-key and
by-id indexes with arbitrary interleaved Put / Invalidate / Touch /
GetByKey / GetById sequences and asserts every invariant that the
recent `invalidate_agent_scoped` fix (a8682e33) was supposed to
establish:

- If by-key returns an entry, by-id must agree on its agent_id (no
  dual-index desync).
- Invalidating by key must clear both indexes (no stale by-id).
- Invalidating by id must clear both indexes (no stale by-key).
- Touches don't change the dual-index shape — only last_seen.
- Cache hits+misses bookkeeping never under-reports total ops.

New test helpers:
- CacheOp enum with five variants (Put / Invalidate / Touch /
  GetByKey / GetById) plus a 1-of-5 Strategy producer that emits
  realistic project/agent-id ranges (1..=4 projects, 1..=200
  agent ids, 0..8 name index into a small fixed pool).
- Proptest harness driving a fresh AgentCache through up to 32
  operations per case, checking invariants after each op rather
  than just at the end so shrinks surface the exact op that broke
  coherency.

No source changes, purely additive. Keeps the regression coverage
for br-pf84h (the bug a8682e33 fixed) honest — without this, we
only have an ad-hoc reproduction.
